model_tasks: manager selects the top five leads
manager tells sales assistant to  contact the leads
sales assistant  sends a note to the manager

merged_tasks.model: manager selects the top five leads

user_text:
The process begins with a manager who checks the open leads. After reviewing the leads, the manager selects the top five leads to focus on. Once the top leads are chosen, the manager instructs the sales assistant to contact these leads.

The process then enters a decision point where it checks if the task of contacting the leads is complete. If it is not complete, the process loops back, and the sales assistant continues to call the customers. Once the sales assistant successfully calls a customer, another decision point is reached.

At this second decision point, if the call results in further action, the sales assistant sends a note to the manager. Upon receiving the note, the manager processes the lead accordingly. After processing, the process returns to the decision point to check if there are more leads to handle.

If there are no more leads to process, the process concludes. This marks the end of the sequence, ensuring that all top leads have been contacted and processed as necessary.
output_text:
The process begins with a manager who checks the open leads. After reviewing the leads, the manager selects the top five leads to focus on. Once the top leads are chosen, the manager instructs a sales assistant to contact these leads.

The process then involves a decision point where it checks if the task of contacting the leads is complete. If it is not complete, the process loops back to the point where the sales assistant is instructed to contact the leads again.

Once the sales assistant begins contacting a customer, two activities occur simultaneously. First, the sales assistant calls the customer. At the same time, the sales assistant sends a note to the manager to inform them about the contact attempt. After the note is sent, the manager processes the lead based on the information received.

These parallel activities converge, and the process checks again if the task of contacting the leads is complete. If it is complete, the process ends. If not, the loop continues, and the sales assistant may need to contact the leads again until the task is fully completed. Once all necessary contacts are made and processed, the process concludes.
